reason: Skill-system scope and pre-development checklist for chain planning
Canonical skill-test schema and draft-first export contract for chain metadata mapping
Runtime context for prompt/tool execution boundaries that lifecycle chains must not silently change
Chain planning spans helper skill, backend export, and UI labels across layers
Clarified chain metadata destination, fail-closed boundaries, dependency mapping, validation authority, and Phase 1 inheritance semantics

Closed follow-up review gaps by defining inheritance semantics, confirm-time broken-chain hints, whole-chain export blocking for missing environment setup, and an explicit planning-only UI disclaimer for Phase 1 chains.

Synced lifecycle-chain planning into the shared skill-test spec: chain row fields, environmentContractRef/environmentSource semantics, inheritance boundaries, confirm-time topology validation, and the Phase 1 planning-only runner disclaimer.
Implemented lifecycle-chain row normalization, confirm/export topology validation, Phase 1 export dependency mapping to dependsOnCaseIds, and planning-only UI messaging.
Surfaced chat-exported source metadata in Skill Tests, including chain id tags when lifecycle-chain planning metadata is present, and kept skipped Phase 1 rows visible in export summaries.
Finished the lifecycle-chain workbench panel with @ts-check-safe DOM/button/toast typing so the planning/export UI now passes the full typecheck gate.

Polished lifecycle-chain export metadata handling by removing chain/environment machine fields from case note text while preserving chainPlanning in source_metadata_json, and by documenting skipped full/execution rows as retained for future Phase export.
